channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Night vision view from helicopter gives wild perspective of viral Vanderbilt celebration with goalpost
item.description: The party was on in Nashville after unranked Vanderbilt defeated No. 1 Alabama on Saturday night, and night vision from a helicopter above caught it all.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lynx return to finals with 88-77 win over Sun in Game 5 behind Collier's 27 points, 11 rebounds
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — Napheesa Collier had 27 points and 11 rebounds and Courtney Williams contributed 24 points and both-ends-of-the-court energy to lead the Minnesota Lynx past the Connecticut Sun 88-77 and into the WNBA Finals for the first time in seven years on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: DAVE CAMPBELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pennsylvania high school football player collapses during game following hard hit to helmet
item.description: The family of a Pennsylvania high school football player says a "severe helmet hit on the field" left Carter Mason with a "brain bleed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Phillies one loss from elimination after 7-2 NL Division Series loss to Mets
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Six months of success for the Philadelphia Phillies could be wiped out before the leaves start to turn. With one more loss to the New York Mets, a 95-win regular season would become an afterthought.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thieves slipped watch off Dodgers pitcher Walker Buehler's wrist, police say
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Thieves surrounded Los Angeles Dodgers starting pitcher Walker Buehler last month outside a horse racing track in a Los Angeles suburb and slipped an expensive watch off his wrist as he jostled through the crowd, police said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Brawl at Alabama high school football game results in quarterback being dismissed, parents charged: report
item.description: A brawl between parents after a high school football game in Alabama has resulted in the dismissal of a team quarterback and charges for two involved in the altercation, reports say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Georgia university leaders ask NCAA to ban transgender women from sports
item.description: The regents who govern Georgia’s public universities and colleges want the NCAA to ban transgender women from participating in women’s sports.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Amy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Jets owner Woody Johnson pleads with Haason Reddick to end holdout: 'You'll fit right in'
item.description: New York Jets owner Woody Johnson had to answer for his decision to fire head coach Robert Saleh, but he also implored Haason Reddick to "get in your car, drive down 95" to end holdout.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: MLB will air local games for Guardians, Brewers and Twins next season
item.description: Major League Baseball announced Tuesday it will produce and distribute local broadcasts for the Cleveland Guardians, Milwaukee Brewers and Minnesota Twins next year. All three teams had contracts with Diamond Sports Group that expired at the end of the regular season.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Back at practice in full, Warriors’ Wiggins motivated to rejoin the fold
item.description: The Warriors have big hopes for Andrew Wiggins, who returned to practice as a full participant Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Danny Emerman, The Mercury News

item.title: Patriots will go with first-round draft pick Drake Maye and bench QB Jacoby Brissett, AP source says
item.description: FOXBOROUGH, Mass. (AP) — The New England Patriots are planning to give first-round draft pick Drake Maye his first pro start in the hopes of ending a four-game losing streak under veteran journeyman quarterback Jacoby Brissett, a person with knowledge of the decision told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: JIMMY GOLEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dodgers' Dave Roberts thinks Manny Machado threw ball at him in dugout 'with intent' after 'unsettling' video
item.description: After seeing video of the incident, Los Angeles Dodgers manager Dave Roberts believes San Diego Padres' Manny Machado threw a ball that nearly hit him in dugout with intent
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jerry Jones and Roger Penske partner to give IndyCar a brand new street racing spectacle
item.description: When IndyCar approached the Dallas Cowboys about potentially hosting a street course race in Texas, Jerry Jones saw that Roger Penske was involved and had no hesitation in striking a deal.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Caitlin Clark's new boss to change her team's brand to be 'like Apple' amid WNBA pros calling its fans racist
item.description: The Fever's new president of basketball and business operations, Kelly Krauskopf, said that she wants to make the Fever's brand like the tech company Apple.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Luis Tiant, the charismatic Cuban who pitched the Red Sox to the brink of a championship, dies at 83
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Luis Tiant, the charismatic Cuban with a horseshoe mustache and mesmerizing windup who pitched the Red Sox to the brink of a World Series championship and pitched himself to the doorstep of the baseball Hall of Fame, has died. He was 83.
item.dc:creator: JIMMY GOLEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US Ryder Cup captain Bradley says security will be watching if Bethpage Black crowd gets nasty
item.description: U.S. Ryder Cup captain Keegan Bradley offered a warning Tuesday about Bethpage Black that does not need a special sign by the first tee: Organizers will have security inside the ropes to make sure the New York crowd doesn't get out of control.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hurricane Milton forces Bucs and Lightning to leave Florida early and other teams to alter games
item.description: The NFL's Tampa Bay Buccaneers and NHL’s Tampa Bay Lightning have left Florida ahead of Hurricane Milton to practice the rest of the week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Spain and Barcelona great Iniesta announces retirement after 22-year career
item.description: Andrés Iniesta, who scored Spain's World Cup-winning goal in 2010 and was one of the key players who made Barcelona's tiki-taka thrive for so long, announced his retirement from soccer on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: TALES AZZONI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Saints quarterback Derek Carr leaves with oblique injury in another losing night against the Chiefs
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Saints quarterback Derek Carr was having another tough time against the Chiefs even before he took a wicked shot from blitzing defensive back Chamarri Conner on Monday night, not only ending his game but also New Orleans' hopes of a comeback.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tossed balls from stands, apparently aimed at Profar, interrupt Padres' win in NLDS Game 2
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Tempers flared on the field and in the stands at Dodger Stadium, with rowdy fans tossing baseballs, apparently at San Diego left fielder Jurickson Profar, and then trash that caused a 12-minute wait between pitches during the seventh inning of the Padres' 10-2 win in NL Division S
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Baseball fans discuss Dodgers’ chances in NLDS Game 2 over donuts
item.description: After a rollercoaster of emotions in Game 1 of the National League Divisional Series, baseball lovers were up early getting donuts in Inglewood and eager to share their thoughts with the news on Sunday's matchup between the Los Angeles Dodgers and the San Diego Padres.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Real Salt Lake clinches home playoff game with 1-0 victory
item.description: SAN JOSE, Calif. (ABC4 Sports) - Real Salt Lake will be home for the playoffs, at least in the first round. RSL (15-7-11) extended its five-game unbeaten run with a 1-0 win away at San Jose, as Zac MacMath recorded his second straight clean sheet in goal.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Cal WBB player makes a splash with sneaker design
item.description: BERKELEY, Calif. (KRON) -- Hooper by day, artist by night. That's life for Cal Berkeley women's basketball player Jayda Noble. The senior guard has balanced her nonstop basketball schedule with her other passion -- sneaker design.
item.dc:creator: Jason Dumas, KRON4

item.title: Here's where SoFi ranks among NFL's best (and worst) stadiums
item.description: SoFi Stadium in Inglewood is not the best stadium in the NFL but it did crack the top five, according to a survey released Thursday. Lumen Field, the home of the Seattle Seahawks, was named the best stadium for football fans to visit in USA Today's rankings of all NFL stadiums.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KTLA 5

item.title: Patrick Fishburn ready to end season on high note
item.description: When the Black Desert Championship tees off next week in St George, former Fremont High School, and BYU star Patrick Fishburn will be among the golfers playing in the event.
item.dc:creator: Wesley Ruff, ABC 4

item.title: Indiana Fever star Caitlin Clark a near-unanimous choice as WNBA's Rookie of the Year
item.description: NEW YORK (AP/WGN) — Caitlin Clark has been named the WNBA Rookie of the Year in a near-unanimous vote, giving the Indiana Fever back-to-back winners after Aliyah Boston won the honor last season.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Utah State Volleyball cancels match against San Jose State, becomes fourth team to do so
item.description: The Utah State women's volleyball team has canceled their Oct. 23 match against San Jose State becoming the fourth team this year to do so, according to reports and tweets from state officials. This comes amid a swirling controversy over a SJSU player's gender.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Mahon, ABC 4

item.title: How two Central football players are working to get recruited
item.description: A pair of local high school football stars -- Central High School's Jaime Padilla and Antonio Reyes -- are doing everything they can, even traveling the country to show college football programs what they can do.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Joe Musgrove exits early with apparent injury in Braves-Padres game
item.description: San Diego Padres right-hander Joe Musgrove and Atlanta Braves left-hander Max Fried both had early exits due to apparent injuries Wednesday night in Game 2 of their NL Wild Card Series.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Team president Jed Hoyer disappointed about 2024, but optimistic about Cubs' direction
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — The 2024 MLB playoffs began Tuesday, and the Cubs were once again not among the six teams in the National League playoff field.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
